q: A camera is an optical instrument that forms and records an ___ of an object.
In a camera, a ___ focuses the light from the object to form a real inverted image.
Which component is placed behind the convex lens to control the amount of light energy falling on the film?
The ___ is a circular opening through which the light passes.
The $f$-number of a camera is the ratio of the ___ of the lens to the diameter of aperture.
When taking a photograph in bright light, a ___ $f$-number must be used.
A ___ controls the time during which light enters the camera through the lens.
The shutter speed is expressed in terms of the ___.
The shorter the exposure time, the ___ the shutter speed is.
The ___ consists of a thin layer of chemical compounds of silver.
In a digital camera, the electronic sensor is made up of millions of tiny ___ which respond to light.
The smaller the $f$-number, the ___ the aperture is.
When taking a photograph in dim light, a ___ $f$-number (wide aperture) must be used.
Which part of the camera allows the distance between the lens and film to be adjusted for a sharp image?
The ___ opens and closes the eye, making it similar to the lens cover of a camera.
The front portion of the eye is covered by a tough, transparent membrane called the ___.
The region between the cornea and the eyelens is filled with a transparent liquid called the ___.
Which part of the eye is the colored portion that controls the amount of light energy entering the eye?
The pupil automatically ___ in dim light and contracts in bright light.
The ciliary muscles can vary the ___ of the eyelens to obtain a sharp image on the retina.
When near objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles ___.
When distant objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles ___ and the eyelens becomes less convex.
The ___ is the light sensitive area of the cells at the back of the eye.
The retina consists of ___ and cones.
The ___ consists of cones exclusively and is used for viewing very small objects.
The spot on the retina which has no rods or cones is called the ___.
The image of an object formed on the retina is real, inverted and ___ than the size of the object.
Farsightedness is a defect of vision in which ___ objects are seen clearly.
For a person with farsightedness, rays from near objects intersect at a point ___ the retina.
Farsightedness can be corrected by the use of spectacles with ___ lens.
Nearsightedness is a defect of vision in which ___ objects are seen clearly.
For a person with nearsightedness, the rays from distant objects intersect at a point ___ of the retina.
Nearsightedness can be corrected by the use of spectacles with ___ lens.
The least distance of distinct vision is about ___ for people with normal vision.
For normal vision, the maximum distance of distinct vision is ___.
When objects at infinity are viewed, the eyes are ___.
A microscope is an optical instrument which uses lenses to make ___ images of small objects.
A compound microscope has at least two convex lenses: an ___ lens and an eyepiece lens.
The lens nearer the object in a compound microscope is called the ___.
The objective in a compound microscope produces an image which is real, inverted and ___.
The final image formed by a compound microscope is ___ and on the same side as the object.
The magnifying power ($MP$) of a compound microscope is the ___ of the magnification of objective and eyepiece.
For a large magnifying power, both the objective and the eyepiece should have ___ focal lengths.
The focal length of the objective in a compound microscope should be ___ than that of the eyepiece.
Which type of microscope employs electron beams in place of light?
Optical microscopes use photons, while electron microscopes use ___.
Electron microscopes deliver a more ___ image compared to optical microscopes.
The two main types of electron microscope are Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) and ___.
An optical instrument used for viewing distant objects is called a ___.
The refracting astronomical telescope consists of an objective of ___ and an eyepiece of short focal length.
Which scientist invented a refracting telescope?
The final image formed by the eyepiece in an astronomical telescope is ___ and inverted.
To obtain sharp images of distant objects, the objective of a telescope should have a large ___.
The reflecting telescope was first suggested by ___.
In the terrestrial telescope, a ___ lens is used as an eyepiece to produce an erect final image.
Total reflecting prisms are used in ___ to reduce the length of the terrestrial telescope.
Binoculars consist of a pair of telescopes, one for each ___.
LASER is an acronym for Light Amplification by ___ Emission of Radiation.
A laser is a source of a narrow beam of intense, monochromatic, ___ light.
Which element of a laser concentrates the light to stimulate the emission of laser radiation?
The process of supplying energy to the laser medium to excite it to emit light is called ___.
In ___ emission, the transition occurs when an atom in its excited state interacts with an incident photon.
For stimulated emission to occur, a ___ would be needed.
Coherence means that the light waves a laser contains are in ___ with one another.
One unique property of laser light is that it is ___ (one color).
Lasers are used in medical applications such as ___ surgery.
Laser ___ are used to collect information from the bar code printed on products.
Fibre optics works on the principle of ___.
An optical fibre is composed of two concentric layers called the ___ and cladding.
The refractive index of the core of an optical fibre is slightly ___ than that of the cladding.
Total internal reflection occurs if the angle of incidence is ___ than the critical angle.
___ is a non-surgical procedure to examine the interior of a hollow organ using optical fibres.
Because of refraction and ___, the light beam in an optical fibre attenuates over a distance.
A ___ is a scientific instrument used to separate and measure spectral components.
The collimated beam from a light source is dispersed by a dispersion element such as a ___ or grating.
In a spectrophotometer, a dispersion element separates light into a ___.
The change in absorption and emission of light intensity with ___ allows identification of materials.
___ is the science of measuring visible light weighted according to human eye sensitivity.
The human eye is more sensitive to ___ of 555 nm compared to other colors.
Luminous flux (luminous power) $\Phi$ is measured in ___.
The SI basic unit for luminous intensity $I_v$ is ___.
Luminous intensity is the measure of luminous flux emitted per unit ___.
A solid angle is expressed in ___ in the SI system.
The solid angle subtended by the whole surface area of a sphere is ___ steradian.
___ $L_v$ is the luminous intensity per unit area of light traveling in a given direction.
The SI unit for illuminance is ___.
One lux is equal to one ___.
Reading areas in school study halls should have a minimum average illuminance of ___.
According to the inverse square law of illuminance, $E_v$ is inversely proportional to the ___.
